title: Introduction to OSP Engineering Finance
subhead: Welcome to Skarion Engineering. We design the physical infrastructure that connects America.

chunks.title: The Skarion Approach
chunks.bodyHtml:
You have just been hired as a Staff Accountant at Skarion Engineering, an Outside Plant (OSP) and GIS engineering firm designing fiber networks across the US.

We don't manufacture widgets. We sell expertise and time. Accounting for a project-based services firm is entirely different from what you learn in a textbook.

chunks.title: The Lifecycle of an OSP Project

steps.title: 1. Intake
steps.desc: We receive ROW shapefiles from the client and scope the job.

steps.title: 2. HLD & LLD
steps.desc: Engineers use AutoCAD and Vetro to draft the High-Level and Low-Level Designs. (This is our WIP).

steps.title: 3. Permitting
steps.desc: We submit permit packages to the DOT and municipalities.

steps.title: 4. Delivery & Billing
steps.desc: We deliver the final construction-ready splice packages and recognize revenue.

chunks.title: What Employers Actually Want
chunks.bodyHtml:
When hiring an accountant for an engineering firm, hiring managers look for specific skills:

  • Project-Based Accounting: Can you allocate software costs (like ArcGIS) to specific client projects?
  • WIP Tracking: Can you calculate the value of a fiber design that is only 50% complete at month-end?
  • Advanced Excel: Can you VLOOKUP thousands of labor hours from our offshore drafting team?

callout.html: Understanding Revenue Recognition (when a project is actually 'done' and billable) is the hardest part of this job.

chunks.intro: Let's see if you understand our business model.
chunks.title: Market Reality Check

questions.q: What is the primary 'product' that Skarion Engineering sells?
questions.explanation: We are a services firm. We don't lay the cable; we deliver the blueprints (HLD, LLD, permits) so the construction crews can build it.
